description: Helen Wylie is Director General for the Digital and Transformation Group at the Department for Work and Pensions ( DWP ). She was appointed to this role in May 2025. Helen is also Chief Digital and Information Officer ( CDIO ) at DWP , a role she took up in November 2024 and retains as part of her current remit. Helen joined DWP in 2018. Prior to becoming CDIO , Helen held various Digital Director roles across DWP , most latterly as Chief Technology Officer, and played a key role in DWP ’s wider service modernisation agenda, helping to shape the organisation for the future. Before this, Helen worked in a range of senior technology and digital delivery roles across both the public and private sectors, including at the Bank of England, Experian, and TNT Post Group. Helen is strongly people focused and passionate about improving the vital public services DWP provides.

details.body:
Helen Wylie is Director General for the Digital and Transformation Group at the Department for Work and Pensions (DWP). She was appointed to this role in May 2025.

Helen is also Chief Digital and Information Officer (CDIO) at DWP, a role she took up in November 2024 and retains as part of her current remit.

Helen joined DWP in 2018. Prior to becoming CDIO, Helen held various Digital Director roles across DWP, most latterly as Chief Technology Officer, and played a key role in DWP’s wider service modernisation agenda, helping to shape the organisation for the future.

Before this, Helen worked in a range of senior technology and digital delivery roles across both the public and private sectors, including at the Bank of England, Experian, and TNT Post Group. Helen is strongly people focused and passionate about improving the vital public services DWP provides.

Helen Wylie is Director General for the Digital and Transformation Group at the Department for Work and Pensions (DWP). She was appointed to this role in May 2025.

Helen is also Chief Digital and Information Officer (CDIO) at DWP, a role she took up in November 2024 and retains as part of her current remit.

Helen joined DWP in 2018. Prior to becoming CDIO, Helen held various Digital Director roles across DWP, most latterly as Chief Technology Officer, and played a key role in DWP’s wider service modernisation agenda, helping to shape the organisation for the future.

Before this, Helen worked in a range of senior technology and digital delivery roles across both the public and private sectors, including at the Bank of England, Experian, and TNT Post Group. Helen is strongly people focused and passionate about improving the vital public services DWP provides.

The Chief Digital and Information Officer is responsible for: 

  • co-creating and evolving modern, secure, user-centred services 
  • designing and building technology and utilising data to deliver solutions 
  • stability and resilience of the department’s digital estate, which includes: 
  • core products for each service line (for example, Jobseeker’s Allowance Payment System, Pensions Service Computer System) 
  • common products which each interact with core products (for example, Customer Information System, Central Payments System) 
  • data collection, storage, linkage and analysis capability including automation and artificial intelligence 
  • shared infrastructure including desktops, telephony, data centres and networks

role_appointments.title: Helen Wylie - Chief Digital and Information Officer

details.body:
Helen Wylie is Director General for the Digital and Transformation Group at the Department for Work and Pensions (DWP). She was appointed to this role in May 2025.

Helen is also Chief Digital and Information Officer (CDIO) at DWP, a role she took up in November 2024 and retains as part of her current remit.

Helen joined DWP in 2018. Prior to becoming CDIO, Helen held various Digital Director roles across DWP, most latterly as Chief Technology Officer, and played a key role in DWP’s wider service modernisation agenda, helping to shape the organisation for the future.

Before this, Helen worked in a range of senior technology and digital delivery roles across both the public and private sectors, including at the Bank of England, Experian, and TNT Post Group. Helen is strongly people focused and passionate about improving the vital public services DWP provides.

The Director General, Digital and Transformation is responsible for:

  • leading DWP’s strategic transformation
  • owning and delivering the Department’s change portfolio
  • Designing and delivering DWP’s digital services
  • running DWP’s IT estate
